lb_trust_store.description: Name of the trust store. If omitted, Terraform will assign a random, unique name. This name must be unique per region, per account, can have a maximum of 32 characters, must contain only alphanumeric characters or hyphens, and must not begin or end with a hyphen.
lb_trust_store.name: name

lb_trust_store.description: Creates a unique name beginning with the specified prefix. Conflicts with `name`. Cannot be longer than 6 characters.
lb_trust_store.name: name_prefix

lb_trust_store.description: Whether to create a trust store revocation for use with an application load balancer.
lb_trust_store.name: create_trust_store_revocation

lb_trust_store.description: Region where the resource(s) will be managed. Defaults to the Region set in the provider configuration
lb_trust_store.name: region

lb_trust_store.description: Map of tags to assign to the resource.
lb_trust_store.name: tags
revocation_lists

lb_trust_store.description: Controls if resources should be created.
lb_trust_store.name: create

lb_trust_store.description: S3 bucket name holding the client certificate CA bundle.
lb_trust_store.name: ca_certificates_bundle_s3_bucket

lb_trust_store.description: S3 object key holding the client certificate CA bundle.
lb_trust_store.name: ca_certificates_bundle_s3_key

lb_trust_store.description: Version ID of CA bundle S3 bucket object, if versioned, defaults to latest if omitted.
lb_trust_store.name: ca_certificates_bundle_s3_object_version

root.description: Create, update, and delete timeout configurations for the load balancer
root.name: timeouts

root.description: Determines if a security group is created
root.name: create_security_group

root.description: Web Application Firewall (WAF) ARN of the resource to associate with the load balancer
root.name: web_acl_arn

root.description: Indicates whether the two headers (`x-amzn-tls-version` and `x-amzn-tls-cipher-suite`), which contain information about the negotiated TLS version and cipher suite, are added to the client request before sending it to the target. Only valid for Load Balancers of type `application`. Defaults to `false`
root.name: enable_tls_version_and_cipher_suite_headers

root.description: The ID of the customer owned ipv4 pool to use for this load balancer
root.name: customer_owned_ipv4_pool

root.description: Indicates whether to allow a WAF-enabled load balancer to route requests to targets if it is unable to forward the request to AWS WAF. Defaults to `false`
root.name: enable_waf_fail_open

root.description: Whether zonal shift is enabled
root.name: enable_zonal_shift

root.description: A list of security group IDs to assign to the LB
root.name: security_groups

root.description: A list of subnet mapping blocks describing subnets to attach to load balancer
root.name: subnet_mapping

root.description: The name of the LB. This name must be unique within your AWS account, can have a maximum of 32 characters, must contain only alphanumeric characters or hyphens, and must not begin or end with a hyphen
root.name: name

root.description: Indicates whether the Application Load Balancer should preserve the Host header in the HTTP request and send it to the target without any change. Defaults to `false`
root.name: preserve_host_header

root.description: Map of listener configurations to create
root.name: listeners

root.description: Identifier of the VPC where the security group will be created
root.name: vpc_id

root.description: If true, the LB will be internal. Defaults to `false`
root.name: internal

root.description: The IPAM pools to use with the load balancer
root.name: ipam_pools

root.description: Creates a unique name beginning with the specified prefix. Conflicts with `name`
root.name: name_prefix

root.description: Map containing access logging configuration for load balancer
root.name: connection_logs

root.description: Determines how the load balancer handles requests that might pose a security risk to an application due to HTTP desync. Valid values are `monitor`, `defensive` (default), `strictest`
root.name: desync_mitigation_mode

root.description: Indicates how traffic is distributed among the load balancer Availability Zones. Possible values are any_availability_zone (default), availability_zone_affinity, or partial_availability_zone_affinity. Only valid for network type load balancers
root.name: dns_record_client_routing_policy

root.description: If `true`, deletion of the load balancer will be disabled via the AWS API. This will prevent Terraform from deleting the load balancer. Defaults to `true`
root.name: enable_deletion_protection

root.description: Default port used across the listener and target group
root.name: default_port

root.description: Determines how the load balancer modifies the X-Forwarded-For header in the HTTP request before sending the request to the target. The possible values are `append`, `preserve`, and `remove`. Only valid for Load Balancers of type `application`. The default is `append`
root.name: xff_header_processing_mode

root.description: Default protocol used across the listener and target group
root.name: default_protocol

root.description: Map of additional target group attachments to create. Use `target_group_key` to attach to the target group created in `target_groups`
root.name: additional_target_group_attachments
root.type:
map(object({
    target_group_key  = string
    target_id         = string
    target_type       = optional(string)
    port              = optional(number)
    availability_zone = optional(string)
  }))

root.description: Security group egress rules to add to the security group created
root.name: security_group_egress_rules
root.type:
map(object({
    name = optional(string)

    cidr_ipv4                    = optional(string)
    cidr_ipv6                    = optional(string)
    description                  = optional(string)
    from_port                    = optional(string)
    ip_protocol                  = optional(string, "tcp")
    prefix_list_id               = optional(string)
    referenced_security_group_id = optional(string)
    tags                         = optional(map(string), {})
    to_port                      = optional(string)
  }))

root.description: A map of additional tags to add to the security group created
root.name: security_group_tags

root.description: Do you agree that Putin doesn't respect Ukrainian sovereignty and territorial integrity? More info: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Putin_khuylo!
root.name: putin_khuylo

root.description: Indicates whether HTTP headers with header fields that are not valid are removed by the load balancer (`true`) or routed to targets (`false`). The default is `true`. Elastic Load Balancing requires that message header names contain only alphanumeric characters and hyphens. Only valid for Load Balancers of type `application`
root.name: drop_invalid_header_fields

root.description: Determines whether the security group name (`security_group_name`) is used as a prefix
root.name: security_group_use_name_prefix

root.description: Security group ingress rules to add to the security group created
root.name: security_group_ingress_rules
root.type:
map(object({
    name = optional(string)

    cidr_ipv4                    = optional(string)
    cidr_ipv6                    = optional(string)
    description                  = optional(string)
    from_port                    = optional(string)
    ip_protocol                  = optional(string, "tcp")
    prefix_list_id               = optional(string)
    referenced_security_group_id = optional(string)
    tags                         = optional(map(string), {})
    to_port                      = optional(string)
  }))

root.description: Map of Route53 records to create. Each record map should contain `zone_id`, `name`, and `type`
root.name: route53_records

root.description: Indicates whether a Web Application Firewall (WAF) ACL should be associated with the load balancer
root.name: associate_web_acl

root.description: Controls if resources should be created (affects nearly all resources)
root.name: create

root.description: Client keep alive value in seconds. The valid range is 60-604800 seconds. The default is 3600 seconds
root.name: client_keep_alive

root.description: Map containing health check logging configuration for application load balancers
root.name: health_check_logs

root.description: The time in seconds that the connection is allowed to be idle. Only valid for Load Balancers of type `application`. Default: `60`
root.name: idle_timeout

root.description: Minimum capacity for a load balancer. Only valid for Load Balancers of type `application` or `network`
root.name: minimum_load_balancer_capacity

root.description: Name to use on security group created
root.name: security_group_name

root.description: The type of load balancer to create. Possible values are `application`, `gateway`, or `network`. The default value is `application`
root.name: load_balancer_type

root.description: A map of tags to add to all resources
root.name: tags

root.description: Indicates whether HTTP/2 is enabled in application load balancers. Defaults to `true`
root.name: enable_http2

root.description: Indicates whether the X-Forwarded-For header should preserve the source port that the client used to connect to the load balancer in `application` load balancers. Defaults to `false`
root.name: enable_xff_client_port

root.description: Indicates whether inbound security group rules are enforced for traffic originating from a PrivateLink. Only valid for Load Balancers of type network. The possible values are on and off
root.name: enforce_security_group_inbound_rules_on_private_link_traffic

root.description: A list of subnet IDs to attach to the LB. Subnets cannot be updated for Load Balancers of type `network`. Changing this value for load balancers of type `network` will force a recreation of the resource
root.name: subnets

root.description: Map of target group configurations to create
root.name: target_groups
root.type:
map(object({
    connection_termination = optional(bool)
    deregistration_delay   = optional(number)
    health_check = optional(object({
      enabled             = optional(bool)
      healthy_threshold   = optional(number)
      interval            = optional(number)
      matcher             = optional(string)
      path                = optional(string)
      port                = optional(string)
      protocol            = optional(string)
      timeout             = optional(number)
      unhealthy_threshold = optional(number)
    }))
    ip_address_type                    = optional(string)
    lambda_multi_value_headers_enabled = optional(bool)
    load_balancing_algorithm_type      = optional(string)
    load_balancing_anomaly_mitigation  = optional(string)
    load_balancing_cross_zone_enabled  = optional(string)
    name                               = optional(string)
    name_prefix                        = optional(string)
    port                               = optional(number)
    preserve_client_ip                 = optional(bool)
    protocol                           = optional(string)
    protocol_version                   = optional(string)
    proxy_protocol_v2                  = optional(bool)
    slow_start                         = optional(number)
    stickiness = optional(object({
      cookie_duration = optional(number)
      cookie_name     = optional(string)
      enabled         = optional(bool)
      type            = string
    }))
    tags = optional(map(string))
    target_failover = optional(list(object({
      on_deregistration = string
      on_unhealthy      = string
    })))
    target_group_health = optional(object({
      dns_failover = optional(object({
        minimum_healthy_targets_count      = optional(string)
        minimum_healthy_targets_percentage = optional(string)
      }))
      unhealthy_state_routing = optional(object({
        minimum_healthy_targets_count      = optional(number)
        minimum_healthy_targets_percentage = optional(string)
      }))
    }))
    target_health_state = optional(object({
      enable_unhealthy_connection_termination = bool
      unhealthy_draining_interval             = optional(number)
    }))
    target_type = optional(string)
    target_id   = optional(string)
    vpc_id      = optional(string)
    # Attachment
    create_attachment = optional(bool, true)
    availability_zone = optional(string)
    # Lambda
    attach_lambda_permission  = optional(bool, false)
    lambda_qualifier          = optional(string)
    lambda_statement_id       = optional(string)
    lambda_action             = optional(string)
    lambda_principal          = optional(string)
    lambda_source_account     = optional(string)
    lambda_event_source_token = optional(string)
  }))

root.description: Description of the security group created
root.name: security_group_description

root.description: Region where the resource(s) will be managed. Defaults to the Region set in the provider configuration
root.name: region

root.description: Map containing access logging configuration for load balancer
root.name: access_logs

root.description: If `true`, cross-zone load balancing of the load balancer will be enabled. For application load balancer this feature is always enabled (`true`) and cannot be disabled. Defaults to `true`
root.name: enable_cross_zone_load_balancing

root.description: The type of IP addresses used by the subnets for your load balancer. The possible values are `ipv4` and `dualstack`
root.name: ip_address_type

lb_trust_store.description: Map of revocation lists and their attributes.
lb_trust_store.name: revocation_lists

lb_trust_store.description: ARN suffix for use with cloudwatch metrics.
lb_trust_store.name: trust_store_arn_suffix

root.description: Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the security group
root.name: security_group_arn

root.description: ID of the security group
root.name: security_group_id

root.description: The DNS name of the load balancer
root.name: dns_name

root.description: Map of target groups created and their attributes
root.name: target_groups

root.description: The Route53 records created and attached to the load balancer
root.name: route53_records

root.description: The ID and ARN of the load balancer we created
root.name: id

root.description: The ID and ARN of the load balancer we created
root.name: arn

root.description: ARN suffix of our load balancer - can be used with CloudWatch
root.name: arn_suffix

root.description: The zone_id of the load balancer to assist with creating DNS records
root.name: zone_id

root.description: Map of listeners created and their attributes
root.name: listeners

root.description: Map of listeners rules created and their attributes
root.name: listener_rules
